title: Ranunculus tureholmiensis
taxonomy: Ranunculus tureholmiensis is classified within the family Ranunculaceae and belongs to the genus Ranunculus, which consists of approximately 600 species of flowering plants. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of China. Further classification places Ranunculus tureholmiensis within the section Ranunculus, which includes a large number of species. The exact classification of Ranunculus tureholmiensis can be broken down into the following hierarchy: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, order Ranunculales, family Ranunculaceae, genus Ranunculus, and species R. tureholmiensis. The classification of this species is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Ranunculus tureholmiensis, also known as the Tureholm buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the ranunculaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the northern hemisphere. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus tureholmiensis include ranunculin, a toxic alkaloid with potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids with antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including wounds, eczema, and joint pain.
